hiddenReason: Danish kommunes don't fund policing (national service) and don't fund the same scope as Canadian municipalities even after normalization, so the % comparison is structurally noisy. Reactivate when the page methodology supports cross-system comparison.
contextNote: Danish kommuner fund K-12 schools, eldercare, and social services Canadian municipalities don't — plus police is a national service. Even normalized to Canadian-comparable spending, too many scope differences remain for a clean side-by-side.
dataStatusNote: Verified from Esbjerg Kommune's Budget 2025-2028 agreement: total kommune budget 9.7 billion DKK; new school masterplan investment 105 mio. DKK over 2025-28; adult social area strengthened by +40 mio. DKK/year. Like Karlskrona, Danish kommuner fund services Canadian municipalities don't: K-12 schools (Skoler), preschool/daycare (Dagtilbud), eldercare (Ældreomsorg), social services and unemployment transfers (Social & Arbejdsmarked). Those are excluded so percentages reflect a Canadian-comparable scope (~2.23 billion DKK). Police is national in Denmark, so public-safety is fire/rescue (Beredskab) only — typically a tiny share. Per-bucket amounts within the normalized scope are proportional estimates pending line-item transcription from the official kommune budget PDF.

excludedCategories.name: Børne- og Familieudvalget (children, family, K-12 schools, daycare/dagtilbud)
Sundheds- og Omsorgsudvalget (health and eldercare / ældreomsorg)
Social- og Arbejdsmarkedsudvalget (social services, unemployment transfers, integration)

normalization.note: Excluded so percentages reflect a Canadian-comparable municipal scope. The 'totalOperating' field shows the un-normalized total (9.7 Bn DKK) for transparency; bucket shares are computed against 'totalOperatingNormalized' (2.23 Bn DKK). Police is national in Denmark, not municipal.

nativeDepartments.name: Beredskab (fire/rescue — typically Sydvestjysk Brandvæsen regional service) (estimated)
Note: police is a national service in Denmark — not in the municipal budget
Teknik- og Byggeudvalget (roads, traffic, public buildings, maintenance) (estimated)
Run by DIN Forsyning A/S (the publicly-owned Esbjerg-Varde utility company), separate from the kommune's tax-supported budget
Kultur- og Fritidsudvalget (culture, libraries, sports facilities, parks, swimming pools) (estimated)
Plan- og Byudviklingsudvalget (planning, urban development, building permits, environment) (estimated)
